Bearing 315

The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club
Fourth Quarter 2003 | www.CascadeOC.org | Page 1

![Logo of Cascade Orienteering Club: A square graphic depicting a compass rose inside a box, next to the bold text "Bearing 315"]

Current COC Officers and Coordinators

  • *Vice President – Eugen Groff, 206-985-2198, groffmarek@earthlink.net

  • E-punch Coordinator – Jennifer Knowles
  • *Patrick Kelly, 206-760-9554, patandlaureen@onebox.com

* Executive Board Member

COC Annual Meeting November 22

This year's Annual Meeting will take place after the WIOL 2 event (at Marymoor Park, Redmond). We hope for a big attendance. Besides the election of the 5 board members, we will be voting on a potential increase in our membership dues. This will also be a social event, with pizza provided by the club (buy your own drinks and other food).

Time: 2 pm (lunch), 3 pm (meeting)
Place: BIG TIME Pizzeria, 7824 Leary Way NE, Redmond

Directions

Directions from Marymoor Park: exit on the west side of the park. Follow W Lake Sammamish Pkwy north under Highway 520. Turn right (east) at the next stoplight onto Leary Way. After crossing the Railroad track and just before Cleveland St. you will see a red brick house on your right. There's a big parking lot in the back of the restaurant; enter from Leary or Cleveland (max. 5 minutes drive from the park!)

From Highway 520: take the exit to W Lake Sammamish Parkway and follow the above directions.

From the east: follow Redmond Way west. Turn left onto Leary Way and go one block to Cleveland St. (Cleveland is one-way eastbound). Big Time Pizzeria is on the corner of Cleveland and Leary Way. There's a big parking lot in the back of the restaurant, enter from Leary or Cleveland.

Board Elections

The following people are running for 2004 Board of Directors positions. You may either vote at the annual meeting or mail in the ballot you should have received in the mail.

  • Don Atkinson
  • Don Denbo
  • Peter Golde
  • Eugen Groff
  • Katie Sprugel

![Photo of Bill Cusworth smiling and holding his award plaque from USOF]

Bill Cusworth Honored by USOF

Congratulations to Bill Cusworth, COC's Technical Coordinator, who was recently honored by USOF for his work on the USOF annual rankings. You can see the product of Bill's efforts on his web site at:
http://students.washington.edu/run/rankings/

Mapping Plans

Plans are underway to produce a new map in the Salmon La Sac area, near Cle Elum Lake in eastern Washington. We plan to use this map for an "A" meet in the summer of 2005. We are currently negotiating with a mapper from the east coast to spend some time this summer doing the fieldwork and computer cartography. We also plan to maximize our investment by having him work on updating the Lord Hill Regional Park map near Monroe. This park has great potential for orienteering and we've used it a couple of times already, but it has never been properly mapped.

Vampire-O' - Woodland Park, October 26

Meet Director: Patrick Kelly
Course Setter: Eileen Breseman
Registration: Kean Williams, James Siscel, Dave Parsons
Start: Patrick Kelly
Finish: Eugen Groff, Eileen Breseman, Jim Siscel, Ginger Stratton, Don Atkinson
Marker Pickup: Peter Golde, Rick Breseman, Ben Hall
Refreshments: Pat and Eileen

It was a beautiful fall night. We had 65 groups or individuals attend. Perhaps as many as 200 participants. Control point #20 was removed before the start, so everyone was given credit for that point. Also, due to a "log jam" at the finish, those finishing in 47 minutes or less were not penalized.

block_index.text: The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club Fourth Quarter 2003 | www.CascadeOC.org | Page 1
![Logo of Cascade Orienteering Club: A square graphic depicting a compass rose inside a box, next to the bold text "Bearing 315"]

This year's Annual Meeting will take place after the WIOL 2 event (at Marymoor Park, Redmond). We hope for a big attendance. Besides the election of the 5 board members, we will be voting on a potential increase in our membership dues. This will also be a social event, with pizza provided by the club (buy your own drinks and other food).
Time: 2 pm (lunch), 3 pm (meeting) Place: BIG TIME Pizzeria, 7824 Leary Way NE, Redmond

Directions from Marymoor Park: exit on the west side of the park. Follow W Lake Sammamish Pkwy north under Highway 520. Turn right (east) at the next stoplight onto Leary Way. After crossing the Railroad track and just before Cleveland St. you will see a red brick house on your right. There's a big parking lot in the back of the restaurant; enter from Leary or Cleveland (max. 5 minutes drive from the park!)
From Highway 520: take the exit to W Lake Sammamish Parkway and follow the above directions.

From the east: follow Redmond Way west. Turn left onto Leary Way and go one block to Cleveland St. (Cleveland is one-way eastbound). Big Time Pizzeria is on the corner of Cleveland and Leary Way. There's a big parking lot in the back of the restaurant, enter from Leary or Cleveland.
The following people are running for 2004 Board of Directors positions. You may either vote at the annual meeting or mail in the ballot you should have received in the mail.
![Photo of Bill Cusworth smiling and holding his award plaque from USOF]

Congratulations to Bill Cusworth, COC's Technical Coordinator, who was recently honored by USOF for his work on the USOF annual rankings. You can see the product of Bill's efforts on his web site at: http://students.washington.edu/run/rankings/

Plans are underway to produce a new map in the Salmon La Sac area, near Cle Elum Lake in eastern Washington. We plan to use this map for an "A" meet in the summer of 2005. We are currently negotiating with a mapper from the east coast to spend some time this summer doing the fieldwork and computer cartography. We also plan to maximize our investment by having him work on updating the Lord Hill Regional Park map near Monroe. This park has great potential for orienteering and we've used it a couple of times already, but it has never been properly mapped.
Canadian Short Race Championships, August 22, Garden Creek North
Canadian Orienteering Championships, August 23/24, Garden Creek
Meet Director, Vetter, Timing: Harvey Friedman Course Designer: Pat Kelly Registration: Pat Kelly
The overcast day kept the temperature low but it probably also scared away all the other folks disappointed that they couldn't go to the canceled meet in Teanaway area the previous day.
Course 2 "Cherchez les churches" (5.8 km, 9 controls) As the name suggests, only churches were used as controls for this course.
U.S. Champs September 13/14, S. Lake Tahoe, CA
National Orienteering Day, Woodland Park, Seattle - September 20

Meet Director: Peter Golde Course Setter: Eugen Groff Registration: Dave Petty, Anna Urbanova, Matej Urban, Don Atkinson Start: Anne York, Eugen Groff, Peter Golde Finish: Sandy Miller, Don Denbo, Anne York Instruction: Harvey Friedman, Robert Miller, Mike Schuh
North Seattle Community College, Seattle - October 5

Co-Meet Directors: Eric Bone, Don Atkinson Courses: Eric Bone Starts: Doug Hathaway Registration: Jim Siscel, Mark Howlett Finishes: Paul Shanahan, Don Atkinson Marker pickup/setout: Eric Bone, Mark Howlett, Annaliese Steuben
Participants enjoyed combinations of a three-loop set of courses on the new North Seattle Community College map produced by Eric Bone.
Bo Powell Memorial, Fort Casey State Park, Whidbey Island - October 19

Meet Director/Course Designer: Bill Cusworth E-punch (finish timing): Jennifer Knowles, Tori Campbell Finish help: Eric Bone Starts: Don Atkinson Set-up: Jennifer Knowles, Don Atkinson Registration: Heidi Cusworth, Jana Dvorak, Anna Urbanova, Matej Urban, A.J. Ritter Beginner Instruction: Eric Bone Traffic guards: Terry Farrah, Matej Urban, Dave Petty, Eugen Groff, A.J. Ritter Control Pick-up: Jennifer Knowles, Terry Farrah

Meet Director: Patrick Kelly Course Setter: Eileen Breseman Registration: Kean Williams, James Siscel, Dave Parsons Start: Patrick Kelly Finish: Eugen Groff, Eileen Breseman, Jim Siscel, Ginger Stratton, Don Atkinson Marker Pickup: Peter Golde, Rick Breseman, Ben Hall Refreshments: Pat and Eileen

It was a beautiful fall night. We had 65 groups or individuals attend. Perhaps as many as 200 participants. Control point #20 was removed before the start, so everyone was given credit for that point. Also, due to a "log jam" at the finish, those finishing in 47 minutes or less were not penalized.
Sally Riggers, Anita Moore, Elena Bishop
Katie Arlan, Carolyn Birnbaum, Rolf, Sue, Holly Kuestner
The following had more penalty points than Controls
Kim Voros, Allison Gilespie, Miles & Jessee Worz (15 controls) - 51:04
Liz, Buck, Pierce and Courtney Brown (10 controls) - 55:15
Finally, the three who ended the event as vampires: Peter Cohen, Miguel Goyela, Jon Connolly
Six Degrees of Navigation Adventure Race, St. Edward State Park, Kenmore - August 30
